type_tip: For table completion, locate the specific item and then listen for the relevant numerical information.
scan_target: There's one place called Bingleys that looks possible. It's $19.75 a night.
analysis_logic: Match the name 'Bingley's' with the mentioned price for a night to fill the budget gap.

type_tip: For table completion, identify the specific descriptive word that fits the context of the blank.
scan_target: They have theme nights every weekend, whatever that means.
analysis_logic: Listen for the type of 'nights' offered regularly by the cafe.

type_tip: For table completion, listen for descriptive adjectives that modify the given noun.
scan_target: It looks as if it's a bit out of town, says it's got an alpine setting, a quiet alpine setting.
analysis_logic: Find 'Chalet Lodge' and listen for the adjective describing its 'alpine setting'.

type_tip: For table completion, pay attention to specific groups of people mentioned as being welcome.
scan_target: Oh, and it says it's children friendly.
analysis_logic: Listen for a group of people explicitly stated to be welcome or for whom the place is friendly.

type_tip: For table completion, identify specific services or meals that are stated as included in the price.
scan_target: And that includes breakfast.
analysis_logic: After locating 'Globetrotters,' listen for what is mentioned as an inclusion in the cost.

type_tip: For table completion, note any unique opportunities or prizes offered by the accommodation.
scan_target: Oh, and it says something about a free skydive! Wow! ... actually, what it says is you can win a chance to do a skydive.
analysis_logic: Listen for any special offers, specifically what can be won, to complete the comment.

type_tip: For classification questions, listen for direct expressions of willingness or refusal from each speaker.
scan_target: Jacinta: I suppose we have to do a bungee jump. ... Lewis: You can, if you really want to jump off the side of a bridge with an elastic rope tied round your ankles. I'll watch.
analysis_logic: Jacinta implies participation, while Lewis explicitly states he will 'watch,' indicating only Jacinta is keen.

type_tip: For classification questions, identify if both speakers express enthusiasm or agreement towards the activity.
scan_target: Lewis: He said it was really awesome. ... Jacinta: Oh, I'm on for that if you are.
analysis_logic: Lewis describes it positively, and Jacinta agrees to do it, suggesting both are interested.

type_tip: For classification questions, distinguish between merely mentioning an activity and expressing a personal desire to do it.
scan_target: Jacinta: That sounded just a lot of noise though. ... Lewis: He said it was a bit touristy, but worth it. I'll give it a go.
analysis_logic: Jacinta expresses a negative opinion, while Lewis states he 'will give it a go,' indicating only Lewis is willing.

type_tip: For classification questions, note if both speakers plan or discuss preparations for the activity.
scan_target: Jacinta: We ought to do some trekking. Scenery around there's amazing. I don't want to miss that. ... Lewis: I'll pack my walking boots. I'd better start getting in training.
analysis_logic: Jacinta expresses a desire to trek, and Lewis plans to prepare for it, confirming mutual interest.

type_tip: For multiple-choice questions, listen for the speaker's main point about the current situation, not historical context.
scan_target: You're approaching the point where a whole generation of people have grown up not being allowed by their parents to cycle, because it was considered to be getting too dangerous. And so in turn, they can't teach their children.
analysis_logic: The key phrase 'not being allowed by their parents to cycle' and 'can't teach their children' indicates a lack of experience rather than foolishness or risk-taking.

type_tip: For multiple-choice questions, focus on the specific method or approach described by the organization.
scan_target: We aim to train them by teaching them to ride on the actual roads they'll use, so they can develop the basic skills they need and build up their confidence that way.
analysis_logic: The strategy is to train them 'on the actual roads they'll use,' directly matching option A.

type_tip: For multiple-choice questions, identify the specific challenges or environments these advanced cyclists want to master.
scan_target: That's for urban cyclists who want to deal with things like riding in streets with complicated intersections and things like that.
analysis_logic: The mention of 'complicated intersections' points to navigating 'complex road systems.'

type_tip: For multiple-choice questions, pay attention to what the organization 'promotes' or 'believes' regarding cycling safety.
scan_target: We believe the key to safe cycling is assertiveness. Taking your place on the road.
analysis_logic: The emphasis on 'assertiveness' and 'taking your place on the road' means riding confidently on busy roads.

type_tip: For list completion, listen for distinct categories of organizations mentioned in sequence.
scan_target: For example, we can deliver fun, safe cycle training activities at schools...
analysis_logic: The first specific type of organization mentioned is 'schools.'

type_tip: For list completion, identify other organizational bodies that receive services from CitiCyclist.
scan_target: ...we have provided training courses for employees and staff of local councils.
analysis_logic: The second distinct organizational client mentioned is 'local councils.'

type_tip: For list completion, be alert for synonyms or related terms for institutional clients.
scan_target: And we are also increasingly looking at developing training courses in companies...
analysis_logic: The third type of organization highlighted is 'companies.'

type_tip: For note completion, extract the exact numerical contact information provided.
scan_target: You can either phone us on 020 7562 4028...
analysis_logic: Locate the phrase 'phone us on' and transcribe the following number.

type_tip: For note completion, identify the cost associated with a single individual for the service.
scan_target: We charge 27 pounds 50 per hour for one-to-one lessons...
analysis_logic: The phrase 'one-to-one lessons' refers to a single person, so note the price given.

type_tip: For note completion, note the standard duration mentioned, specifically excluding special cases.
scan_target: And for most people a course of road training usually takes three hours.
analysis_logic: The phrase 'for most people' indicates the usual length, excluding beginners as specified in the question.

type_tip: For short-answer questions, listen for direct statements of agreement or shared positive opinion between the speakers.
scan_target: Sharon: ...I think that our technique was generally good. ... Tutor: So, you both feel happiest about that side of the presentation.
analysis_logic: Sharon identifies 'technique' as good, and the tutor confirms this shared positive feeling.

type_tip: For short-answer questions, identify specific parts of the presentation that a speaker indicates they were 'least happy with' or had 'problems' with.
scan_target: The conclusion wasn't too bad, but the problem was the questions. We hadn't really expected there'd be any.
analysis_logic: Xiao Li explicitly states 'the problem was the questions,' making it clear what he was least happy with.

type_tip: For short-answer questions, listen for suggestions for improvement or areas where a speaker felt they were 'weak' or lacked 'evaluation'.
scan_target: I thought the section on solutions seemed rather weak. ... I suppose we didn't really evaluate any of the projects or ideas.
analysis_logic: Sharon labels the 'solutions' section as 'weak' and notes the lack of evaluation, suggesting it needed more depth.

type_tip: For multiple-choice questions, identify the specific feedback that was unexpected or contradictory to the students' perception.
scan_target: They said sometimes it was a bit hard to understand, because we were talking quite fast. But we didn't think so when we watched the video.
analysis_logic: The students' surprise is highlighted by their disagreement with the feedback that they 'spoke too quickly.'

type_tip: For multiple-choice questions, look for instances where the class provided contrasting or opposing opinions on a single aspect.
scan_target: Some people said that we could have had more on the slides... But other people said the slides were good.
analysis_logic: The 'some people said... but other people said' structure indicates conflicting feedback on their 'use of visuals' (slides).

type_tip: For multiple-choice questions, identify what made their presentation unique compared to the 'most other people'.
scan_target: But one thing that did come out was that they liked the fact we'd done research on both Northern Ireland and China. Most other people had just based their research on one country.
analysis_logic: The distinguishing factor was researching 'both Northern Ireland and China,' which means their data collection was more wide-ranging than just one country.

type_tip: For multiple-choice questions involving visuals, carefully match the spoken scores to the corresponding categories in the chart.
scan_target: I'm giving you full marks for content. Five. ... The structure of the presentation was good, but not quite as good as the content. So, I gave that four. And the same for technique.
analysis_logic: Content = 5, Structure = 4, Technique = 4. Match these scores to the correct bar chart.

type_tip: For sentence completion, listen for the tutor's direct commentary on the presentation's conclusion.
scan_target: I think your ending was rather abrupt. You suddenly just stopped talking.
analysis_logic: The tutor uses 'abrupt' as a synonym for 'sudden' and refers to the 'ending' of the presentation.

type_tip: For sentence completion, identify specific feedback from the tutor that highlights a positive aspect of their results discussion.
scan_target: I felt a very strong point was that after you'd given your results, you explained their limitations.
analysis_logic: The tutor praises the explanation of the 'limitations' of the results.

type_tip: For sentence completion, listen for the tutor's suggestions for expanding or improving a particular section of the report.
scan_target: But maybe you could flesh out the literature review a bit.
analysis_logic: To 'flesh out' means to extend or elaborate, referring to the 'literature review'.

type_tip: For note completion, locate the WHO's criteria and identify the adjectives used to describe the environment.
scan_target: The WHO says that amongst other things, a healthy city must provide a clean environment which is also safe.
analysis_logic: The criteria specify a 'clean environment' that is also 'safe.'

type_tip: For note completion, identify the fundamental requirements a healthy city must provide for its residents.
scan_target: The WHO says a healthy city has got to be able to satisfy its inhabitants' basic needs.
analysis_logic: The phrase 'satisfy its inhabitants' basic needs' directly addresses the blank.

type_tip: For note completion, listen for what the WHO recommends regarding community involvement in governance.
scan_target: The WHO says this is something that the whole community should be involved in... local government.
analysis_logic: The text states the 'whole community should be involved in' aspects of 'local government'.

type_tip: For table completion, identify the main group or entity responsible for carrying out the method in the project.
scan_target: But what was different about it was that the residents did this. The people who actually lived there, not people from outside.
analysis_logic: The text clearly states 'the residents did this,' referring to the construction of infrastructure.

type_tip: For table completion, identify the specific type of opportunities that were improved as a result of the project.
scan_target: And this meant that not only did the people end up with improved housing and infrastructure, but also... it improved their chances from an economic point of view.
analysis_logic: The phrase 'from an economic point of view' directly leads to 'economic opportunities.'

type_tip: For table completion, identify the specific group of people involved in organizing the garbage collection method.
scan_target: In this case, the cooperative involved students who had graduated from secondary school in getting a system going.
analysis_logic: The method involved 'students who had graduated from secondary school.'

type_tip: For table completion, identify the specific media or activities used for the public education campaign.
scan_target: The cooperative set up a campaign to educate the public about the importance of good sanitation through showing films and setting up discussion groups...
analysis_logic: One of the methods used for the campaign was 'films,' alongside discussion groups.

type_tip: For table completion, identify the type of center where the project was primarily based.
scan_target: This project was based in a women's center in a poor area called Makatam.
analysis_logic: The project's base was a 'women's center'.

type_tip: For table completion, identify what the women were taught or given to enable them to make products.
scan_target: At the women's center, they were shown how to sew and how to weave. And once they'd learned these skills, they were given the equipment...
analysis_logic: They were taught 'skills' like sewing and weaving, and then given equipment.

type_tip: For table completion, identify the specific social or personal improvements that resulted from the project.
scan_target: And this project has meant that these young women have greater status in the community... they can enjoy a better quality of life.
analysis_logic: The project resulted in 'greater status' in the community and a better quality of life.
